title: Giving Opinions Without Overstating
description: 今天练习表达观点但不过度绝对，听起来更成熟自然.

items.cn: 比 I think 更有判断感，但不武断.
examples: My read is that the team needs more time.
My read is that pricing isn't the main issue.
My read is that we should keep this simple.
dialogue: What's your take on the feedback?
My read is that people like the idea but need clearer examples.

items.cn: 表达偏好但留有讨论空间.
examples: I'm leaning toward the second option.
I'm leaning toward staying in tonight.
I'm leaning toward pushing this to next week.
dialogue: I'm leaning toward the cleaner one.

examples: I'm not totally convinced this solves the problem.
I'm not totally convinced we need a new tool.
I'm not totally convinced by that argument yet.
dialogue: Should we rebuild the whole page?
I'm not totally convinced that's necessary.

items.phrase: It depends on what we're optimizing for.
items.cn: 适合复杂讨论，显得很清醒.
examples: It depends on what we're optimizing for: speed or quality.
It depends on what we're optimizing for here.
It depends on what matters most to the client.
dialogue: It depends on what we're optimizing for.

items.cn: 降低语气强度，适合提出不同看法.
examples: I could be wrong, but I think we missed a step.
I could be wrong, but that doesn't sound right.
I could be wrong, but she seemed upset.
dialogue: I could be wrong, but I think the dates don't line up.
